name: Miners Heritage Walk-In Mine
note: The world's largest underground walk-in sapphire tourist mine, offering guided tunnel tours and buckets of 'wash' to fossick for your own sapphires.

name: Bobby Dazzler Sapphire Mine Tours
note: Underground sapphire mine tours (marked by Rubyvale's Big Miner statue) plus fossicking wash for hunting your own gems.

name: Pat's Gems Fossicking Park
note: A long-running gemfields fossicking park where visitors buy a bag of wash and sieve for sapphires and zircons with staff guidance.

name: Sapphire Caravan & Cabin Park Fossicking
note: A caravan park in the Sapphire gemfields selling a $10 bag of wash so first-timers can find their own sapphire.

name: Rubyvale/Sapphire Designated Fossicking Land
note: Government-designated fossicking land (Sapphire, Scrub Lead, Divide, Rubyvale and Reward) where licence-holders can dig for world-class sapphires, rubies and zircon.

name: The Willows Gemfields Fossicking Park
note: A popular gemfields township (with fossicking park and designated land) known for yellow and green sapphires, including the famous 332-carat 'Golden Willow'.

name: Tomahawk Creek Fossicking Area
note: A government fossicking area in the Central Queensland gemfields where licence-holders dig for sapphire and zircon.

name: Glenalva Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area in the Central Queensland gemfields for recreational sapphire and zircon digging.

name: Middle Ridge Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area in the Willows section of the gemfields for sapphire and zircon.

name: Big Bessie Fossicking Area
note: A government-designated fossicking area in the Central Queensland gemfields for sapphire and zircon.

name: Graves Hill Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area in the gemfields where licence-holders can dig for sapphires and zircon.

name: Thunderbird Park Thunderegg Mine
note: The world's largest thunderegg mine, where visitors dig 200-million-year-old volcanic thundereggs filled with jasper, quartz and agate.

name: Swipers Gully Topaz Locality
note: A government fossicking locality near Stanthorpe where licence-holders search for topaz.

name: Thanes Creek Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area near Warwick where visitors can pan for gold.

name: Deep Creek Fossicking Area
note: A government fossicking area near Warwick set aside for recreational gold panning.

name: Talgai State Forest Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area in state forest near Warwick where visitors search for gold.

name: Durikai State Forest Fossicking Area
note: A government fossicking area in state forest where licence-holders can pan for gold.

name: Chinchilla Petrified Wood Localities
note: Designated fossicking localities near Chinchilla where visitors collect colourful petrified (fossilised) wood.

name: Lightning Ridge Public Fossicking (Noodling) Heaps
note: Free public noodling heaps in Australia's black-opal capital where visitors sift mullock piles by hand for opal.

name: Grawin Opal Fields Fossicking Tours
note: Public opal dump sites and guided full-day tours to the Grawin, Sheepyard and Glengarry opal fields with fossicking on working mine dumps.

name: White Cliffs Opal Fields
note: A historic outback opal field where visitors can fossick or 'spec' for opal on the surface with landowner permission.

name: Fossicking in Oberon (Designated Areas)
note: Public designated fossicking areas around Oberon where visitors dig for sapphires and zircons.

name: Nundle Fossicking (Peel River & Reserves)
note: Historic goldfield with public sites at Swamp Creek Reserve, Hanging Rock and the Peel River for panning gold and sapphires.

name: Staggy Creek Fossicking Area
note: A designated fossicking area 28km from Inverell where visitors search for diamonds, topaz, black tourmaline and quartz.

name: Bullock Mountain Homestead Fossicking
note: A homestead 'pay to dig' venue on Reddestone Creek where guests fossick for sapphire and zircon in the Glen Innes highlands.

name: Yarrow Creek Bridge Fossicking Site
note: A public roadside fossicking site in the Glen Innes highlands, part of the region famous for royal-blue sapphires.

name: Wellingrove Bridge Fossicking Site
note: A public fossicking site in the Glen Innes highlands where visitors sieve creek gravels for sapphire.

name: Torrington State Conservation Area Fossicking
note: A conservation area (permit required) with sites like Blatherarm Creek where visitors fossick for topaz, quartz and beryl.

name: Emmaville & Vegetable Creek Fossicking
note: Historic 'Vegetable Creek' mineral field near the Emmaville Mining Museum where visitors fossick for sapphire, topaz, garnet and beryl.

name: Three Creeks Gold Mine
note: A pay-to-dig gold mine near Bingara offering fossicking and camping with equipment hire on site.

name: Hill End Historic Goldfield
note: A historically rich goldfield township where visitors can still pan and fossick for gold.

name: Coober Pedy Opal Noodling
note: The opal capital of the world, where visitors can noodle by hand through mullock heaps for opal (no permit needed for hand noodling).

name: Andamooka Opal Fields
note: A famous outback opal field where visitors can fossick on spoil heaps and join local tours to the diggings.

name: Jupiter Creek Diggings
note: A popular public gold-fossicking site in the Adelaide Hills where visitors pan and dig in the old Echunga goldfield (no permit required).

name: Watts Gully Diggings, Mount Crawford Forest
note: A ForestrySA-designated fossicking area (permit purchased online) where visitors pan and dig for gold.

name: Gumeracha Goldfields, Mount Crawford Forest
note: A designated forest fossicking area near Gumeracha (ForestrySA permit required) for recreational gold panning.

name: Cowell Jade Province
note: A ~10 sq km area on the Eyre Peninsula holding Australia's largest nephrite jade deposit, associated with the Cowell jade industry.

name: Beechworth Historic Park Prospecting Area
note: A Parks Victoria historic park with designated prospecting areas where miner's-right holders pan and detect for gold.

name: Castlemaine Diggings National Heritage Park
note: A national heritage park of goldrush diggings with designated areas where visitors pan and detect for gold.

name: Chiltern-Mt Pilot National Park Prospecting Area
note: A national park near Beechworth with designated prospecting areas for gold panning and detecting.

name: Greater Bendigo National Park Prospecting Area
note: A national park on the rich Bendigo goldfield with designated areas where visitors pan and detect for fine gold.

name: Enfield State Park Prospecting Area
note: A state park near Ballarat with designated prospecting areas for recreational gold fossicking.

name: Heathcote-Graytown National Park Prospecting Area
note: A national park with designated areas where miner's-right holders prospect for gold.

name: Kara Kara National Park Prospecting Area
note: A national park with designated prospecting areas for gold panning and metal detecting.

name: Kooyoora State Park Prospecting Area
note: A granite state park in the golden triangle with designated areas where visitors detect and pan for gold.

name: Paddys Ranges State Park Prospecting Area
note: A state park near Maryborough with designated prospecting areas for recreational gold fossicking.

name: Steiglitz Historic Park Prospecting Area
note: A former goldrush township park with designated areas where visitors pan and detect for gold.

name: Warrandyte State Park Prospecting Area
note: A state park on the Yarra where Victoria's first gold was found, with designated areas for panning and prospecting.

name: Reef Hills State Park Prospecting Area
note: A state park near Benalla with designated prospecting areas for recreational gold fossicking.

name: Mud Tank Zircon Field
note: A declared public fossicking area off the Plenty Highway where visitors dig for colourful zircon crystals, with bush camping permitted.

name: Gemtree Caravan & Fossicking Park
note: A Plenty Highway park running tag-along garnet and zircon fossicking tours to the Harts Range fields with equipment and guides supplied.

name: Harts Range West Garnet Field
note: A declared fossicking area off the Plenty Highway where visitors collect well-shaped garnet specimens up to a few centimetres.

name: Central Harts Range Fossicking Area (Spotted Tiger)
note: A declared fossicking area including the Spotted Tiger mine and camp, where visitors search for gemstones with camping nearby.

name: Weld River Fossicking Area
note: A declared fossicking area in NE Tasmania where visitors search river gravels for sapphire, topaz, black spinel and quartz (no licence needed).

name: Gladstone (Mount Cameron) Fossicking Area
note: A declared fossicking area around old tin workings near Mount Cameron yielding smoky quartz, amethyst, topaz and jasper.

name: Killiecrankie Bay Fossicking Area
note: A declared fossicking area on Flinders Island yielding topaz ('Killiecrankie diamonds'), smoky quartz and rare beryl.

name: Golden Quest Discovery Trail Prospecting
note: A goldfields self-drive trail through historic rush towns (Coolgardie, Gwalia, Laverton) where miner's-right holders can prospect for gold.

name: Mount Magnet Prospecting
note: WA's longest-surviving gold town, where miner's-right holders can prospect and detect for gold on eligible crown land.

name: Kalgoorlie Goldfields Prospecting
note: The heart of WA's goldfields, where visitors with a miner's right can prospect for gold on eligible unoccupied crown land.

name: Cue & Murchison Goldfields Prospecting
note: A Murchison gold town popular for metal-detecting, where miner's-right holders prospect for gold on eligible crown land.
